localized-strings.localized-string: Terms, definitions, symbols and abbreviated terms
No terms and definitions are listed in this document.
For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply.

The following documents are referred to in the text in such a way that some or all of their content constitutes requirements of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies.
There are no normative references in this document.
For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % apply.
For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % and the following apply.

note.p: Changes to this Product Specification are coordinated by the IHO Nautical Information Provision Working Group (NIPWG). New editions will be made available via the IHO web site. Maintenance of the Product Specification shall conform to IHO Technical Resolution 2/2007 (as amended).

sourcecode.body: - date: - type: published value: 2022-03-11 edition: "1.0.0" contributor: - organization: name: International Hydrographic Organization subdivision: Nautical Information Provision Working Group abbreviation: NIPWG amend: - description: Release 1.0.0 - date: - type: updated value: 2022-05 edition: "1.0.0" contributor: - organization: name: International Hydrographic Organization subdivision: Hydrographic Services and Standards Committee abbreviation: HSSC amend: - description: Initial published version for evaluation and testing. - date: - type: updated value: 2023-10 edition: "1.1.0" contributor: - organization: name: Korea Hydrographic and Oceanographic Agency abbreviation: KHOA amend: - description: Align with S-100 Ed 5.0.0 evaluation and testing. - date: - type: updated value: 2024-02 edition: "1.2.0" contributor: - organization: name: International Hydrographic Organization subdivision: Hydrographic Services and Standards Committee abbreviation: HSSC amend: - description: Initial published version for evaluation and testing. - date: - type: updated value: 2024-03 edition: "2.0.0" contributor: - organization: name: Korea Hydrographic and Oceanographic Agency abbreviation: KHOA amend: - description: Updated to align with S-100 Edition 5.2.0 and Applied feedback from NIPWG - date: - type: updated value: 2024-07 edition: "2.0.0" contributor: - organization: name: Korea Hydrographic and Oceanographic Agency abbreviation: KHOA amend: - description: Applied feedback from NIPWG - date: - type: published value: 2025-03 edition: "2.0.0" contributor: - organization: name: International Hydrographic Organization abbreviation: IHO CL xx/2025 amend: - description: First operational Edition of S-128 Annex A

clause.p: © Copyright International Hydrographic Organization 2025

This work is copyright. Apart from any use permitted in accordance with the Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works (1886), and except in the circumstances described below, no part may be translated, reproduced by any process, adapted, communicated or commercially exploited without prior written permission from the International Hydrographic Organization (IHO). Copyright in some of the material in this publication may be owned by another party and permission for the translation and/or reproduction of that material must be obtained from the owner.

This document or partial material from this document may be translated, reproduced or distributed for general information, on no more than a cost recovery basis. Copies may not be sold or distributed for profit or gain without prior written agreement of the IHO Secretariat and any other copyright holders.
In the event that this document or partial material from this document is reproduced, translated or distributed under the terms described above, the following statements are to be included:

quote.p: “Material from IHO publication [reference to extract: Title, Edition] is reproduced with the permission of the IHO Secretariat (Permission No ……./…) acting for the International Hydrographic Organization (IHO), which does not accept responsibility for the correctness of the material as reproduced: in case of doubt, the IHO’s authentic text shall prevail. The incorporation of material sourced from IHO shall not be construed as constituting an endorsement by IHO of this product.”

“This [document/publication] is a translation of IHO [document/publication] [name]. The IHO has not checked this translation and therefore takes no responsibility for its accuracy. In case of doubt the source version of [name] in [language] should be consulted.”

clause.p: The IHO Logo or other identifiers shall not be used in any derived product without prior written permission from the IHO Secretariat.

feedback-statement.p:
Published by the
International Hydrographic Organization
4b quai Antoine 1er
Principauté de Monaco

Fax: (377) 93.10.81.40

semx.p: Release 1.0.0
Initial published version for evaluation and testing.
Align with S-100 Ed 5.0.0 evaluation and testing.
Initial published version for evaluation and testing.
Updated to align with S-100 Edition 5.2.0 and Applied feedback from NIPWG
Applied feedback from NIPWG
First operational Edition of S-128 Annex A

clause.title: Preface
clause.p: The “Data Classification and Encoding Guide” has been developed to provide consistent, standardized instructions for encoding S-100 compliant Catalogue of Nautical Products (S-128) data.

The purpose of the Data Classification and Encoding Guide is to facilitate S-128 encoding to meet IHO standards for the proper display of Catalogue of Nautical Products information in an ECDIS and other electronic charting displays. This document describes how to encode information that the modeller considers relevant to a Catalogue of Nautical data product. The content of a dataset is at the discretion of the producing authority provided that the conventions described within this document are followed. A “producing authority” is a Hydrographic Office (HO) or other organization authorized by a government, to produce definitive nautical information.
The entire S-100 Universal Hydrographic Data Model, including the S-128 Product Specification, is available at the following web site, http://www.iho.inthttp://www.iho.int.

clause.title: S-128 Annex A — Data Classification and Encoding Guide — Metadata

fmt-title.semx: S-128 Annex A — Data Classification and Encoding Guide — Metadata

note.p: This information uniquely identifies this Annex to the Product Specification and provides information about its creation and maintenance.

dd.p: The International Hydrographic Organization Catalogue of Nautical Products Specification, Annex A — Data Classification and Encoding Guide.
March 2025
English
Unclassified

International Hydrographic Organization (IHO) 4b, quai Antoine 1er
B.P. 445
MC 98011 MONACO CEDEX
Telephone: +377 93 10 81 00
Fax: +377 93 10 81 40
Email: info@iho.intinfo@iho.int
S-128 Annex A
Changes to S-128 Annex A — Data Classification and Encoding Guide are coordinated by the IHO Nautical Information Provision Working Group (NIPWG) and must be made available via the IHO web site.

terms.title: Terms and definitions
terms.p: For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply.

verbal-definition.p: special form of associationassociation that specifies a whole-part relationship between the aggregate (whole) and a component (see composition)

semx.p: special form of associationassociationassociation (Clause 1.3.1.2) that specifies a whole-part relationship between the aggregate (whole) and a component (see composition)

verbal-definition.p: semantic relationship between two or more classifiers that specifies connections among their instances

semx.p: semantic relationship between two or more classifiers that specifies connections among their instances

termnote.p: A binary association is an association among exactly two classifiers (including the possibility of an association from a classifier to itself)

semx.p: named property of an entity

termnote.p: Describes the geometrical, topological, thematic, or other characteristic of an entity

verbal-definition.p: a strong aggregationaggregation; if a container object is deleted than all of its containee objects are deleted as well (that is, containee objects cannot exist without the container object)

semx.p: a strong aggregationaggregationaggregation (Clause 1.3.1.1); if a container object is deleted than all of its containee objects are deleted as well (that is, containee objects cannot exist without the container object)

verbal-definition.p: 1-dimensional geometric primitive, representing the continuous image of a line

semx.p: 1-dimensional geometric primitive, representing the continuous image of a line

termnote.p: The boundary of a curve is the set of points at either end of the curve. If the curve is a cycle, the two ends are identical, and the curve (if topologically closed) is considered to not have a boundary. The first point is called the start point, and the last is the end point. Connectivity of the curve is guaranteed by the “continuous image of a line”

verbal-definition.p: a fixed list of valid identifiers of named literal values. Attributes of an enumerated type may only take values from this list (source: ISO 19136:2007, Geographic information — Geography Markup Language (GML))

semx.p: a fixed list of valid identifiers of named literal values. Attributes of an enumerated type may only take values from this list (source: ISO 19136:2007, Geographic information — Geography Markup Language (GML))
abstraction of real world phenomena

termnote.p: A feature may occur as a type or an instance. The terms “feature type” or “feature instance” should be used when only one is meant

termexample.p: The feature instance named “Eiffel Tower” may be classified with other phenomena into a feature type “tower”

verbal-definition.p: geometric object representing a single, connected, homogeneous element of geometry

semx.p: geometric object representing a single, connected, homogeneous element of geometry

termnote.p: Geometric primitives are non-decomposed objects that present information about geometric configuration. They include points, curves, surfaces

verbal-definition.p: the largest value of the ratio of the linear dimensions of features of a dataset presented in the display and the actual dimensions of the features represented (largest scale) of the scale range of the dataset

semx.p: the largest value of the ratio of the linear dimensions of features of a dataset presented in the display and the actual dimensions of the features represented (largest scale) of the scale range of the dataset

verbal-definition.p: the smallest value of the ratio of the linear dimensions of features of a dataset presented in the display and the actual dimensions of the features represented (smallest scale) of the scale range of the dataset

semx.p: the smallest value of the ratio of the linear dimensions of features of a dataset presented in the display and the actual dimensions of the features represented (smallest scale) of the scale range of the dataset
0-dimensional geometric primitive, representing a position.

termnote.p: The boundary of a point is the empty set

verbal-definition.p: a set of points in geometric space

semx.p: a set of points in geometric space

verbal-definition.p: a subset of the geographic (geo) features that must create a complete non-overlapping coverage of the area of data coverage of an ENC dataset

semx.p: a subset of the geographic (geo) features that must create a complete non-overlapping coverage of the area of data coverage of an ENC dataset

verbal-definition.p: connected 2-dimensional geometric primitive, representing the continuous image of a region of a plane

semx.p: connected 2-dimensional geometric primitive, representing the continuous image of a region of a plane

termnote.p: The boundary of a surface is the set of oriented, closed curves that delineate the limits of the surface

dd.p: Electronic Chart Display and Information System
Electronic Navigational Chart
Geography Markup Language
Hydrographic Office
International Hydrographic Organization
International Maritime Organization
International Organization for Standardization
System Electronic Navigational Chart
Safety of Life at Sea

clause.title: Use of language
clause.p: Within this document:
“Must” indicates a mandatory requirement;
“Should” indicates an optional requirement, that is the recommended process to be followed, but is not mandatory;
“May” means “allowed to” or “could possibly”, and is not mandatory.

clause.title: Maintenance
clause.p: Changes to the Data Classification and Encoding Guide must occur in accordance with the S-128 CNP Product Specification.

clause.title: General
clause.p: The S-128 Data Classification and Encoding Guide describes how data describing the real world should be captured using the types defined in the S-128 Feature Catalogue. It provides the encoding rules and guidance required to create S-128 CNPs. Within the model these sets of characteristics are defined in terms of feature, spatial and information types. A type is defined as a stereotype of class that is used to specify a domain of instances (objects) together with the operations applicable to the objects. A type may have attributes and may be related to other types.
The types used within S-128 are described below. Within this document feature types, information types, associations and attributes appear in bold text.

clause.title: Feature types
clause.p: Feature types contain descriptive attributes and do not contain any geometry (that is, information about the shape and position of a real world entity).

Features have two aspects — feature type and feature instance. A feature type is a class and is defined in a Feature Catalogue. A feature instance is a single occurrence of the feature type and represented as an object in a dataset. A feature instance is located by a relationship to one or more spatial instances. A feature instance may exist without referencing a spatial instance.
S-128 makes use of the following feature types:
Geographic (Geo) feature type — carries the descriptive characteristics of a real world entity.
Aggregated feature type — a relationship between two or more feature types where the aggregation feature is made up of component features.
Cartographic feature type — contains information about the cartographic representation (including text) of real world entities.
Meta feature type — contains information about other features

clause.title: Geometric primitives
clause.p: The allowable geometric primitive for each feature type is defined in the Feature Catalogue. Within this document, allowable primitives are included in the tables containing a description of each feature type. Allowable geometric primitives are point, pointset, curve and surface.
Each spatial value must be referenced by at least one feature instance.

Within this document, allowable primitives are included in the description of each feature type. It is recommended that curves and surface boundaries should not be encoded at a point density greater than 0.3mm at the maximum display scale for the ENC data.

A curve consists of one or more curve segments. Each curve segment is defined as a loxodromic line on WGS84. Long lines may need to have additional coordinates inserted to cater for the effects of projection change.
The presentation of line styles may be affected by curve length. Therefore, the encoder must be aware that splitting a curve into numerous small curves may result in poor symbolization.

clause.title: Information types
clause.p: An information type is an identifiable object that can be associated with features in order to carry information particular to the associated features. An example of the use of an information type may be the requirement to include a note about overhead cables. Information types can also be associated with other information types. This may be done where there is further supplementary information that is relevant to the information type. Information types carry attributes but not geometry.

clause.title: Attributes
clause.p: Attributes may be simple type or complex type. Complex © attributes are aggregates of other attributes that can be simple type or complex type. Simple attributes are assigned to one of 11 types.

The binding of attributes to feature types, the binding of attributes to attributes to construct complex attributes, and attribute multiplicity is defined in the Feature Catalogue. Within this document, the allowable attributes are included in the description of each feature type, as well as the allowable values for enumeration type attributes.

clause.title: Multiplicity
clause.p: In order to control the number of allowed attribute values or sub-attribute instances within a complex attribute, S-100 uses the concept of multiplicity. This defines lower and upper limits for the number of values, whether the order of the instances has meaning and if an attribute is mandatory or not. Common examples are shown in the table below:
Format: MinOccurs, MaxOccurs (if * Infinite) (ordered) — sequential

tr.td: An instance is not required; there can be only one instance.
An instance is required and there must only be one instance.
An instance is not required and there can be an infinite number of instances.
An instance is required and there can be an infinite number of instances.
An instance is required and there can be an infinite number of instances, the order of which has a specific meaning.
Two instances are required and no more than two.

clause.title: Simple attribute types
clause.p: Each simple attribute is assigned to one of 11 types:

dd.p: Enumeration: A fixed list of valid identifiers of named literal values. Attributes of an enumerated type may only take values from this list.

Boolean: A value representing binary logic. The value can be either True or False. The default state for Boolean type attributes (that is, where the attribute is not populated for the feature) is False.
Real: A signed Real (floating point) number consisting of a mantissa and an exponent. The representation of a real is encapsulation and usage dependent.
Integer: A signed integer number. The representation of an integer is encapsulation and usage dependent.
Examples: 29, -65547
Free text: A CharacterString, that is an arbitrary-length sequence of characters including accents and special characters from a repertoire of one of the adopted character sets.

Truncated Date: A truncated date allows a partial date to be encoded as an extension to the ISO 8601 compliant date attribute type values for year, month and day according to the Gregorian Calendar. Character encoding of a date is a string which follows the calendar date format (complete representation, basic format) for date specified by ISO 8601:2004.
Example: 19980918 (YYYYMMDD)
Time: A time is given by an hour, minute and second. Character encoding of a time is a string that follows the local time (complete representation, basic format) format defined in ISO 8601:2004.
Time zone according to UTC is optional. Example: 183059 or 183059+0100 or 183059Z

The complete representation of the time of 27 minutes and 46 seconds past 15 hours locally in Geneva (in winter one hour ahead of UTC), and in New York (in winter five hours behind UTC), together with the indication of the difference between the time scale of local time and UTC, are used as examples.

Date: A date provides values for year, month and day according to the Gregorian Calendar.
Example (XML/GML): 1998-09-18 (YYYY-MM-DD)
Date and Time: A DateTime is a combination of a date and a time type. Character encoding of a DateTime shall follow ISO 8601:2004 (see TD and TI above and clause 2.4.1).
URL: A uniform resource locator (URL) is a URI that provides a means of locating the resource by describing its primary access mechanism (RFC 3986).
URN: A persistent, location-independent, resource identifier that follows the syntax and semantics for URNs specified in RFC 2141.

clause.p: Real or integer attribute values must not be padded by non-significant zeroes. For example, for a signal period of 2.5 seconds, the value populated for the attribute signal period must be 2.5 and not 02.50.

clause.title: Mandatory and conditional attributes
clause.p: Some attributes are mandatory and must be populated for a given feature type. There are some reasons why attribute values may be considered mandatory:

li.p: They are fundamental to the definition of a feature;
They are required to support correct portrayal of a feature instance;
Certain features make no logical sense without specific attributes;
Some attributes are required for safety of navigation.

clause.p:
Within this document, mandatory attributes (multiplicity 1,1; 1,n (
n > 1
n > 1
n > 1
n > 1); or 1,*) are identified in the description of each feature type.

clause.title: Missing attribute values
clause.p: Where a value of a mandatory attribute is not known, the attribute must be populated with an empty (null) value. Where the value of a non-mandatory attribute is not known, the attribute should not be included in the dataset. In a base dataset, when an attribute code is present but the attribute value is missing, it means that the producer wishes to indicate that this attribute value is unknown.
In an Update dataset, when an attribute code is present but the attribute value is missing it means:

li.p: That the value of this attribute is to be replaced by an empty (null) value if it was present in the original dataset; or
That an empty (null) value is to be inserted if the attribute was not present in the original dataset.

clause.title: Textual information
clause.p: The complex attribute information contains information as text using the sub-attribute text, or the name of an external file using the sub-attribute file reference.

Character strings contained in information sub-attribute text must be UTF-8 character encoding. Information should generally be used for short notes or to transfer information which cannot be encoded by other attributes, or to give more detailed information about a feature. Text populated in text must not exceed 300 characters.

The exchange language for textual information should be English, therefore it is not required to populate the sub-attribute language for an English version of textual information. Languages other than English may be used as a supplementary option, for which language must be populated with an appropriate value to indicate the language. Generally this means, when a national language is used in the textual attributes, the English translation must also exist.

tr.td: IHO Definition: An element within a catalogue of elements.

1: ENC
2: bathymetric chart
3: water level product
4: surface current product
5: MSI service
6: AtoN information
7: catalogue service
8: routing service
9: ice information
10: routing information
11: special purpose chart
12: nautical publication
13: printed nautical chart

16: search and rescue service

100: other
IHO Definition: A physical or electronic product, that is primarily intended for navigation.

1: navigational purpose overview

4: navigational purpose approach
5: navigational purpose harbour
6: navigational purpose berthing

37: highest shipping height of water according to danube commission

li.p: Issue Date in S-57 and S-101 ENCs defines the date when the ENC cell edition was issued. The issue date must be greater than the previous issue date of the dataset.

tr.td: IHO Definition: A product printed on paper.
IHO Definition: A service that makes use of S-100 based product specifications to support data transfer.

1: GML

1: default name display
2: alternate name display
IHO Definition: Information about the authority responsible for production.

li.p: The purpose of having separate attributes for agencyName and agencyResponsibleForProduction is to distinguish between the organization responsible for the S-128 dataset’s content (agencyResponsibleForProduction) and the organization that actually carries out the production process (agencyName). This separation allow flexibility in cases where the producing agency acts on behalf of another responsible body, ensuring that both roles are clearly identified.

tr.td: IHO Definition: Information related to a distributor.

td.p: IHO Definition: A carriage requirement required by SOLAS or other regulation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Details related to distribution

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Contact information of distributor

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Mapping between traditional products and S-100 Products.

li.p: No remarks.

tr.td:
1: higher priority alternative
2: lower priority alternative
3: recommended enhancement provider
4 : recommended enhancement user

td.p: IHO Definition: An association of price information to a catalogue element

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The price of a nautical product

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Contact information of producer

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Contact information of a producing organization

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A package or distinct set of products

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A supplementary or secondary part of the product, which may appear multiple times, offering control or display functionalities depending on its configuration

li.p: No remarks.

tr.td: IHO Definition: The top section of a catalogue.
IHO Definition: A container of elements.
IHO Definition: Reference to an element within a catalogue.
IHO Definition: Reference to a Catalogue of Nautical product.
IHO Definition: Information on how to reach a person or organisation by postal, internet, telephone, telex and radio systems.
IHO Definition: Reference to the distributor.
IHO Definition: Reference to an element.
IHO Definition: Reference to price information.
IHO Definition: Reference to a producer.
IHO Definition: Reference to supporting material or information related to a specific element or data.
IHO Definition: Reference to a requirement for a specific system or process.
IHO Definition: Reference to the source of information or data.
IHO Definition: Reference to the main product containing panel(s).
IHO Definition: Reference to the panel of a main product.

td.p: IHO Definition: A generic term for an administrative region within a country at a level below that of the sovereign state.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of an agency, entity or organization.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Identifies the agency which produced the data.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Name of an application profile that can be used with the online resource.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Approximate grid resolution for nautical products.

li.p: A single value may be provided when all axes have a common resolution. For multiple value provision, use axis order as specified in dataset. May be approximate for ungeorectified data. For example, for 5 metre resolution, the value 5 must be encoded. If the grid cell size varies over the extent of the grid, an approximated value based on model parameters or production metadata should be used. For S-111, approximateGridResolution is mandatory for grid formats, thus with multiplicity [1..*]. It is also mandatory for S-104.

td.p: [underline]#IHO Definition:#Classification of a catalogue element.

li.p: IHO Definition: Electronic Navigational Chart.
IHO Definition: A topographic chart of the bed of a body of water, or a part of it. Generally, bathymetric charts show depths by contour lines and gradient tints.
IHO Definition: Water Level Information for Surface Navigation.
IHO Definition: A product representing the water velocity at one or more geographic locations down to a given depth.
IHO Definition: MSI (Maritime Safety Information) service.
IHO Definition: A service providing information related to Marine Aids to Navigation.
IHO Definition: A service providing structured records of items.
IHO Definition: Services associated with Ships Routeing.
IHO Definition: Newly discovered icebergs, changes to ice conditions and ice related information likely to impact navigation.
IHO Definition: Information associated with Ships Routeing.
IHO Definition: Any chart designed primarily to meet specific requirements.

IHO Definition: A (nautical chart or) nautical publication is a “a special-purpose map or book, or a specially compiled database from which such a map or book is derived, that is issued officially by or on the authority of a Government, authorized Hydrographic Office or other relevant government institution and is designed to meet the requirements of marine navigation”.

IHO Definition: A printed nautical chart is a “a special-purpose map, that is issued officially by or on the authority of a Government, authorized Hydrographic Office or other relevant government institution and is designed to meet the requirements of marine navigation”
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Identifier of a catalogue element.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A number identifying a section within a catalogue.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The catalogue section title.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The type of person, government agency or organisation granted powers of managing or controlling access to and/or activity in an area.

li.p: IHO Definition: The administration to prevent or detect and prosecute violations of rules and regulations at international boundaries.
IHO Definition: The department of government, or civil force, charged with maintaining public order.

IHO Definition: Person or corporation, owners of, or entrusted with or invested with the power of managing a port. May be called a Harbour Board, Port Trust, Port Commission, Harbour Commission, Marine Department.
IHO Definition: The authority controlling people entering a country.
IHO Definition: The authority with responsibility for checking the validity of the health declaration of a vessel and for declaring free pratique.
IHO Definition: Organization keeping watch on shipping and coastal waters according to governmental law; normally the authority with responsibility for search and rescue.
IHO Definition: The authority with responsibility for preventing infection of the agriculture of a country and for the protection of the agricultural interests of a country.
IHO Definition: A military authority which provides control of access to or approval for transit through designated areas or airspace.
IHO Definition: A private or publicly owned company or commercial enterprise which exercises control of facilities, for example a calibration area.
IHO Definition: A governmental or military force with jurisdiction in territorial waters. Examples could include Gendarmerie Maritime, Carabinierie, and Guardia Civil.
IHO Definition: An authority with responsibility for the protection of the environment.
IHO Definition: An authority with responsibility for the control of fisheries.
IHO Definition: An authority with responsibility for the control and movement of money.
IHO Definition: A national or regional authority charged with administration of maritime affairs.
IHO Definition: The agency or establishment for collecting duties, tolls.
IHO Definition: State agency in charge of marine surveys and hydrography.
IHO Definition: Regional ENC Coordination Centre.
IHO Definition: Value Added Resellers (VARs), who are able to offer comprehensive end-use services that bring together various navigational products into one package
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A classification of the internal relationships between products and services.

li.p: IHO Definition: A higher prioritized or recommended alternative product or service, that can fully replace another.
IHO Definition: A lower prioritized or not recommended alternative product or service, that can fully replace another.
IHO Definition: A recommended additional product or service, that provides added value to another.
IHO Definition: A product or service, that is recommended to make use of added value provided by another product or service
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Designation of the character set to be used to encode the textual value of the locale.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of a town or city.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Indicates a classification.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Comment regarding an entity obvious from context.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: In ECDIS, the scale at which the data was compiled.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Indicates if the resource is compressed.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Instructions provided on how to contact a particular person, organisation or service.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Definition of a period when a contract is valid.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of a nation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Something (such as coins, treasury notes, and banknotes) that is in circulation as a medium of exchange

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name or identification of a dataset.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The latest date on which an object (for example a buoy) will be present.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The earliest date on which an object (for example a buoy) will be present.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Details of where post can be delivered such as the apartment, name and/or number of a street, building or PO Box.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: [underline]#IHO Definition:#Specifies the algorithm used to compute digital signature value.

li.p: IHO Definition: Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA) that based upon the issuing certificate. It’s signed with the issuer’s key P-384
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Value derived from the digital signature.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Supply status of nautical products.

li.p: IHO Definition: A product or service that is currently in production.
IHO Definition: A product or service that has been withdrawn
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Name of the distributor.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A carriage requirement that is specific to a country or region and is based on domestic legislation or regulation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Date of publishing for example of a publication, chart, or product.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Edition of the ENC being referenced

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Expiration date of a product or service.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The location of a fragment of text or other information in a support file.

li.p: Application schemas must describe how the associated file is identified. The associated file will commonly be named in a file reference co-attribute of the same complex attribute. Each DCEG must specify requirements for the format of the associated file and the semantics of file locator. For example, the value of file locator may be an HTML ID in an HTML file, line number in a text file) or a bookmark in a PDF file.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of a file within a system.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The file name of an externally referenced text file.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Words set at the head of a passage or page to introduce or categorize.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Horizontal reference as an EPSG code representing a valid entry in the EPSG Geodetic Parameter Dataset, as maintained by the Geodesy Subcommittee of the IOGP Geomatics Committee, and provided online at epsg.org.
IHO Definition: A global Mercator projection commonly used for mapping applications requiring accurate distance measurements near the equator.
IHO Definition: A popular web mapping projection used by Google Maps, OpenStreetMap, and Bing Maps. Distorts at the poles but is widely used in online maps.
IHO Definition: World Geodetic System 1984, used globally for GPS and geographic coordinates. Specifies coordinates in latitude and longitude degrees

li.p: codeListType=open enumeration; encoding=other: [something]

td.p: IHO Definition: A maritime service as identified by the International Maritime Organization (IMO).

li.p: IHO Definition: Any service implemented by a relevant authority primarily designed to improve safety and efficiency of traffic flow and the protection of the environment. It may range from simple information messages, to extensive organization of the traffic involving national or regional schemes.
IHO Definition: A service providing up-to-date information of Aids to Navigation.
IHO Definition: An option that is reserved for future use.
IHO Definition: A service that provides information necessary to organize and support port calls and varies depending on the local needs.
IHO Definition: A service providing navigational and meteorological warnings, meteorological forecasts and other urgent safety-related messages broadcast to ships.

IHO Definition: The services of a person who directs the movements of a vessel through pilot waters, usually a person who has demonstrated extensive knowledge of channels, aids to navigation, dangers to navigation, etc., in a particular area and is licensed for that area, are available.

IHO Definition: A service that contributes to the safety of navigation, protection of the marine environment, and efficiency of marine transportation by conducting different types of operations including tugboats, such as ship assistance, salvage, towage, escort etc.
IHO Definition: A service providing information related to Vessel Shore Reporting and Ship reporting systems.
IHO Definition: A service to provide decision support and advice to the seafarer on board responsible for medical care.

IHO Definition: A service to manage communications between the coastal State, ships’ officers requiring assistance and other responsible maritime organizations: fleet owners, salvage companies, port authorities, brokers, etc.

IHO Definition: A service that provides geospatial information (in digital and / or printed format) to support safe maritime navigation with the aim to fulfill SOLAS regulation V/19.2.1.4 requirements for ships to carry “nautical charts and nautical publications to plan and display the ship’s route for the intended voyage and to plot and monitor positions throughout the voyage”.

IHO Definition: A service to provide information as a support to the navigation process. This comprises information to complement nautical charts, such as information on ports and sea areas, as well as the contact information of authorities and services for a sea area or port. It further describes regulations, restrictions, recommendations and other nautical information applicable in these areas, and aim to fulfill SOLAS regulation V/19.2.1.4 requirements for ships to carry “nautical charts and nautical publications to plan and display the ship’s route for the intended voyage and to plot and monitor positions throughout the voyage”.
IHO Definition: A service to provide ice navigation information to ships in and in the vicinity of possible ice infested regions.
IHO Definition: A service to provide meteorological information (digitally) to ships.

p.strong: real-time hydrographic and environmental information services

li.p: IHO Definition: A service providing hydrographic and environmental observations and forecasts, such as water level and surface current information.
IHO Definition: A service aimed at providing information about and assist with Search and Rescue functions.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: International Carriage requirements are carriage requirements based on the SOLAS-convention or similar international regulation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: International Standard Book Number.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: ISO 216 is a paper-size standard established by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO).

li.p: IHO Definition: The paper size A0, as defined in ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The first size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The paper size A2, as defined in ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The fourth size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The fifth size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The sixth size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The seventh size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
IHO Definition: The eighth size as output size on nautical paper chart. Referring to ISO 216.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: International Standard Serial Number.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Date up to which the data was made available by the Data Producer.

li.p: Issue date in S-57 and S-101 ENCs defines the date when the ENC cell edition was issued. The issue date must be greater than the previous issue date of the dataset.

td.p: IHO Definition: Time of day at which the data was made available by the Data Producer.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The method of human communication, either spoken or written, consisting of the use of words in a structured and conventional way.

li.p: The language is encoded by a 3 character code following ISO 639-3/T.

td.p: IHO Definition: Location (address) for online access using a URL/URI address or similar addressing scheme.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The value considered by the Data Producer to be the maximum (largest) scale at which the data is to be displayed before it can be considered to be “grossly overscaled”

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Thesmallestintendedviewingscaleforthedata.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Theindividualnameof afeature.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Nameoftheonlineresource.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Classification of the type and display level of the name of a feature in an end-user system.

li.p: IHO Definition: The name is intended to be displayed when the end-user system is set to the default name/text display setting.
IHO Definition: The name is intended to be displayed when the end-user system is set to an alternate name/text display setting, for example an alternate language
For ECDIS, all encoded instances of the complex attribute feature name will be able to be viewed in the ECDIS Pick Report, regardless of the value populated for name usage.

td.p: IHO Definition: The navigational purpose of the dataset.

li.p: IHO Definition: For port and near shore operations.
IHO Definition: For coast and planning purposes.
IHO Definition: For ocean crossing and planning purposes
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Indicates the dataset is not intended to be used for navigation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Description of online resources.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The largest intended viewing scale for the data.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The original identification of a product that has been re-branded or distributed under multiple identification schemes.

li.p: Enter Original Chart Number for Original Product Number

td.p: IHO Definition: Description of a support file format other than those listed.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The length in cm of the longer side of a paper.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The length in cm of the shorter side of a paper.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Known in various countries as a postcode, or ZIP code, the postal code is a series of letters and/or digits that identifies each postal delivery area.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The amount of money expected, required, or given in payment for something.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Name of the publishing institution of the paper chart for navigation.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The authority who printed a nautical paper chart.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The authority who produced a nautical product.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Product number of a product or service.

li.p: Encode Chart Number in the Product Number

td.p: IHO Definition: A reference to another product.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Connection protocol to be used.

example.p: ftp, http get KVP, http POST, etc.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Request used to access the resource. Structure and content depend on the protocol and standard used by the online resource, such as Web Feature Service standard.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The official publication date of a notice, product or service.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Publication number of the nautical product.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The date that the item was observed, done, or investigated.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Reprinted version of nautical paper chart.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The authority who reprinted a nautical paper chart.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of a service.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Types of status of services.

li.p: IHO Definition: Indicates a temporary, preliminary, or interim status. A provisional item is not yet finalized or fully approved.
IHO Definition: Indicates a finalized, officially approved, or publicly available status. A released item is ready for general use or distribution.
IHO Definition: Indicates that a feature, method, product, or component is no longer recommended for use but is still available.
IHO Definition: Indicates that a feature, method, product, or component is no longer available or has been permanently removed
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The publication, document, or reference work from which information comes or is acquired.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The production date of the source; for example the date of measurement.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Type of the source.

li.p: IHO Definition: Treaty, convention, or international agreement; law or regulation issued by a national or other authority.
IHO Definition: Publication not having the force of law, issued by an international organisation or a national or local administration.
IHO Definition: Reported by mariner(s) and confirmed by another source.
IHO Definition: Reported by mariner(s) but not confirmed.
IHO Definition: Shipping and other industry publications, including graphics, charts and web sites.
IHO Definition: Information obtained from satellite images.
IHO Definition: Information obtained from photographs.
IHO Definition: Information obtained from products issued by Hydrographic Offices.
IHO Definition: Information obtained from news media.
IHO Definition: Information obtained from the analysis of traffic data.
IHO Definition: A national or regional authority charged with administration of maritime affairs.

td.p: Remarks:

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The use for which the dataset is intended.

li.p: IHO Definition: For use in the study of the characteristics of maritime zones, in the formulation of plans, in the selection of routes, etc., showing only relevant elements of the coastline, harbours, islands, principal navigational marks and obstructions, and submarine landforms.

IHO Definition: A nautical chart with universality (i.e., generality) in use, characterized by the requirement that the chart must comprehensively describe various natural elements and socioeconomic elements, and that each element of the subject matter expressed is universal.
IHO Definition: Used for marine navigation, mainly displaying submarine landforms, navigational marks, navigational obstacles and other elements related to navigation.
IHO Definition: Used for near-shore navigation, mainly showing the marine elements close to coastal areas.
IHO Definition: Used for entering and leaving harbours, selecting anchorage, studying harbour topography, and carrying out the construction of harbours.
IHO Definition: For ships berthing.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The format used for the support file.

li.p: IHO Definition: UTF-8 text excluding control codes.
IHO Definition: JPEG2000 format.
IHO Definition: Hypertext Markup Language.
IHO Definition: Extensible Markup Language.
IHO Definition: Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations.
IHO Definition: A digital recording of an image or set of images (such as a movie or animation).
IHO Definition: Tagged Image File Format (TIFF).
IHO Definition: Portable Document Format.
IHO Definition: Lua programming language.
IHO Definition: Being the one or ones distinct from that or those first mentioned or implied.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The reason for inclusion of the support file.

li.p: IHO Definition: A file which is new.
IHO Definition: A file which replaces an existing file.
IHO Definition: Deletes an existing file
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Anidentifier,suchaswords,numbers,letters,symbols,oranycombinationofthose usedtoestablish acontacttoaparticularperson, organisationorservice.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Classification of methods of communication over a distance by electrical, electronic,orelectromagnetic means.

li.p: IHO Definition: The transfer or exchange of information by using sounds that are being made by mouth and throat when speaking.

IHO Definition: Sometimes called telecopying or telefax (the latter short for telefacsimile), is the telephonic transmission of scanned printed material (both text and images), normally to a telephone number connected to a printer or other output device.
IHO Definition: Short Message Service is a form of text messaging communication on phones and mobile phones.
IHO Definition: Materials that serve as the basis for initiation.
IHO Definition: Data that is constantly received by and presented to an end-user while being delivered by a provider.
IHO Definition: A system of communication in which messages are sent over long distances by using a telephone system and are printed by using a special machine (called a teletypewriter).
IHO Definition: An apparatus, system or process for communication at a distance by electric transmission over wire.
IHO Definition: Messages and other data exchanged between individuals using computers in a network.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A non-formatted digital text string

li.p: Should be used, for example, to hold the information that is for short cautionary or explanatory notes. Therefore, text populated in text must not exceed 300 characters. Text may be in English, or in a national language. No formatting of text is possible within text. If formatted text, or text strings exceeding 300 characters, is required, then an alternate concept should be used.

td.p: IHO Definition: The type of a physical (navigational) product, usually printed on paper

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The type of productformat.

li.p: IHO Definition: Geography Markup Language. An XML-based geographic information encoding language developed by the Open GIS Consortium (OGC) to enhance the interoperability of geographic information.
IHO Definition: Specification for a data descriptive file for information interchange.

IHO Definition: Portable Document Format. A file format developed by Adobe in 1993 to present documents, including text formatting and images, in a manner independent of application software, hardware, and operating systems.
IHO Definition: Hypertext Markup Language.
IHO Definition: E-book file format.

IHO Definition: For printing hydrographic charts, heavyweight, single layer paper is used. Such paper is generally made wholly or partly from rags and simulates hand-made paper. It is strong, moisture resistant and manufactured to withstand surface erasure.
IHO Definition: Hierarchical Data Format version 5 is a file format and data model designed for storing and organizing large amounts of numerical data efficiently.
IHO Definition: A file format used primarily for storing nautical charts in raster form.
IHO Definition: Extension of the TIFF specification to allow the storage of geo-referencing information.
IHO Definition: Provision of data in a format including operational functionality, such as a software program designed to perform specific tasks or functions for the user.
IHO Definition: Extensible Markup Language.
* PNG*
IHO Definition: Portable Network Graphics format.
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The unit of a value indicating a time Time Interval..

li.p: IHO Definition: A unit of time equal to 60 minutes or 3600 seconds.

IHO Definition: The duration of one rotation of the earth, or occasionally another celestial body, on its axis. It is measured by successive transits of a reference point on the celestial sphere over the meridian, and each type takes its name from the reference used.
IHO Definition: A measure of time based on the motion of the moon in its orbit.
IHO Definition: A period of one revolution of the earth around the sun.
This attribute is used for periodically published data, such as S-111. The periodicity is entered as shown in the examples below.
For example, if you want to express 26hrs, you would express it as 1day 2hour.
For example, if you want to express 45days, you would express it as 1month 15hour.
For example, if you want to express 18months, you would express it as 1year 6month.

td.p: IHO Definition: A date referring to the day a product or service was updated.

li.p: Update Date in S-57 and S-101 ENCs defines the date when the latest update for the current ENC cell edition was issued. This field is used whenever there is an update or re-issue of the cell.

td.p: IHO Definition: Update number of the ENC being referenced.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The length or duration of a time interval, referred to a specified time interval unit.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Identification of a specific form or variation of an entity.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The reference level used for expressing the vertical measurements of points on the earth’s surface. Also called datum level, reference plane, levelling datum, datum for sounding reduction, datum for heights.

li.p: IHO Definition: The average height of the low waters of spring tides. This level is used as a tidal datum in some areas.
IHO Definition: The average height of lower low water springs at a place.

IHO Definition: The average height of the surface of the sea at a tide station for all stages of the tide over a 19-year period, usually determined from hourly height readings measured from a fixed predetermined reference level.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level conforming to the lowest tide observed at a place, or somewhat lower.
IHO Definition: The average height of all low waters at a place over a 19-year period.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level conforming to the lowest water level observed at a place at spring tides during a period of time shorter than 19 years.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, usually within 0.3m from that of Mean Low Water Springs (MLWS).
IHO Definition: An arbitrary tidal datum approximating the level of the mean of the lower low water at spring tides. It was first used in waters surrounding India.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, approximating that of mean low water springs (MLWS).
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, usually within 0.3m from that of lowest astronomical tide (LAT).
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level approximating the lowest water level observed at a place, usually equivalent to the indian spring low water (ISLW).
IHO Definition: The average height of the lower low waters at a place over a 19-year period.
IHO Definition: The lowest level reached at a place by the water surface in one oscillation.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, usually within 0.3m from that of mean low water (MLW).
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, usually within 0.3m from that of mean lower low water (MLLW).
IHO Definition: The average height of all high waters at a place over a 19-year period.
IHO Definition: The average height of the high waters of spring tides.
IHO Definition: The highest level reached at a place by the water surface in one oscillation.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, usually within 0.3m from that of mean sea level (MSL).
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level, approximating that of mean high water springs (MHWS).
IHO Definition: The average height of higher high waters at a place over a 19-year period.
IHO Definition: The level of low water springs near the time of an equinox.
IHO Definition: The lowest tide level which can be predicted to occur under average meteorological conditions and under any combination of astronomical conditions..
IHO Definition: An arbitrary datum defined by a local harbour authority, from which levels and tidal heights are measured by this authority.
IHO Definition: A vertical reference system with its zero based on the mean water level at Rimouski/Pointe-au-Père, Quebec, over the period 1970 to 1988.
IHO Definition: The average of all hourly water levels over the available period of record.
IHO Definition: The average of the lowest low waters, one from each of 19 years of observations.
IHO Definition: The average of the highest high waters, one from each of 19 years of observations.
IHO Definition: An arbitrary level approximating the highest water level observed at a place, usually equivalent to the high water springs.
IHO Definition: The highest tidal level which can be predicted to occur under average meteorological conditions and under any combination of astronomical conditions
IHO Definition: Low water reference level of the local area.
IHO Definition: High water reference level of the local area.
IHO Definition: Mean water reference level of the local area.
IHO Definition: A low water level which is the result of a defined low water discharge — called “equivalent discharge”.

p.strong: highest shipping height of water (german HSW)

li.p: IHO Definition: Upper limit of water levels where navigation is allowed.

p.strong: reference low water level according to danube commission

li.p: IHO Definition: The water level at a discharge, which is exceeded 94% of the year within a period of 30 years.

p.strong: highest shipping height of water according to danube commission

li.p: IHO Definition: The water level at a discharge, which is exceeded 1% of the year within a period of 30 years.

p.strong: dutch river low water reference level (OLR)

li.p: IHO Definition: The water level at a discharge, which is exceeded 95% of the year within a period of 20 years.
IHO Definition: Conditional low water level with established probability.
IHO Definition: Highest water level derived from the upper backwater stream in watercourse or reservoir under the normal operational conditions.
IHO Definition: The Ohio River datum.
IHO Definition: Dutch High Water Reference Level.

IHO Definition: The datum refers to each Baltic country’s realization of the European Vertical Reference System (EVRS) with land-uplift epoch 2000, which is connected to the Normaal Amsterdams Peil (NAP).

p.strong: dutch estuary low water reference level (OLW)

li.p: IHO Definition: Dutch Estuary Low Water Reference Level (OLW).

IHO Definition: The 2020 update to the International Great Lakes Datum (IGLD), the official reference system used to measure water level heights in the Great Lakes, connecting channels, and the St. Lawrence River system.
IHO Definition: The bottom of the ocean and seas where there is a generally smooth gentle gradient. Also referred to as sea bed (sometimes seabed or sea-bed), and sea bottom.

IHO Definition: A two-dimensional (in the horizontal plane) field representing the air-sea interface, with high-frequency fluctuations such as wind waves and swell, but not astronomical tides, filtered out.
IHO Definition: A vertical reference near the lowest astronomical tide (LAT), below which the sea level falls only very exceptionally
No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A consecutive number that specifies a week within a year.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A consecutive number that specifies a week within a year.

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Direction or superscription of a letter, package, etc., specifying the name of the place to which it is directed, and optionally a contact person or organisation who should receive it.

dd.p:
administrative division (see Clause 7.1)
city name (see Clause 7.13)
country name (see Clause 7.20)
delivery point (see Clause 7.25)
postal code (see Clause 7.61)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: User specified paper size width x, height y.

dd.p:
paper length (see Clause 7.59)
paper width (see Clause 7.60)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Locale of an option that is selected automatically unless an alternative is specified.

dd.p:
character encoding (see Clause 7.12)
country name (see Clause 7.20)
language (see Clause 7.46)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Provides the name of an entity, defines the national language of the name, and provides the option to display the name at various system display settings.

dd.p:
language (see Clause 7.46)
name (see Clause 7.50)
name usage(see Clause 7.52)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Textual information about the feature. The information may be provided as a string of text or as a file name of a single external text file that contains the text.

dd.p:
file locator (see Clause 7.34)
file reference (see Clause 7.36)
headline (see Clause 7.37)
language (see Clause 7.46)
text (see Clause 7.85)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The cycle of issuing a product or service.

dd.p:
periodic date range (see Clause 8.8)
time interval of cycle (see Clause 8.19)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Information about online sources from which a resource or data can be obtained.

Sub-attributes: application profile (see Clause 7.4)
linkage (see Clause 7.47)
name of resource (see Clause 7.51)
online description (see Clause 7.55)
protocol (see Clause 7.68)
protocol request (see Clause 7.69)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The active period of a recurring event or occurrence.

dd.p:
date end (see Clause 7.23)
date start (see Clause 7.24)

li.p: No remarks

td.p: IHO Definition: A decision or establishment of a price.

dd.p:
contract period (see Clause 7.19)
currency (see Clause 7.21)
price (see Clause 7.62)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Information on the printing of nautical paper charts.

dd.p:
print agency (see Clause 7.63)
print nation (see Clause 7.64)
reprint edition (see Clause 7.73)
reprint nation (see Clause 7.74)
print size (see Clause 8.11)

li.p: No remarks

td.p: IHO Definition: Size of nautical paper charts.

dd.p:
ISO 216 (see Clause 7.42)
custom paper size (see Clause 8.2)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of the product specification to which a nautical product adheres.

dd.p:
edition date (see Clause 7.31)
ISSN (see Clause 7.43)
name (see Clause 7.50)
version (see Clause 7.92)

li.p: No remarks..

td.p: IHO Definition: A reference to a specific Notice to Mariners.

dd.p:
publication date (see Clause 7.70)
week of year (see Clause 8.21)

li.p: The date of the last group of NMs consulted for its correction, even if this group and possibly preceding groups did not in fact contain any updates to be made to the product in question.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of the (product) specification to which a nautical service adheres.

dd.p:
edition date (see Clause 7.31)
name (see Clause 7.50)
version (see Clause 7.92)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Information about the source document, publication, or reference from which object data or textual material included or referenced in a dataset are derived.

dd.p:
category of authority (see Clause 7.10)
country name (see Clause 7.20)
reported date (see Clause 7.72)
source (see Clause 7.77)
source type (see Clause 7.79)
feature name (see Clause 8.4)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: Information on additional files used in addition to nautical products.

dd.p:
comment (see Clause 7.15)
digital signature reference (see Clause 7.26)
digital signature value (see Clause 7.27)
edition number (see Clause 7.32)
file locator (see Clause 7.34)
file name (see Clause 7.35)
issue date (see Clause 7.44)
other data type description (see Clause 7.58)
support file format (see Clause 7.81)
support file purpose (see Clause 7.82)
default locale (see Clause 8.3)
support file specification (see Clause 8.17)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The name of the product specification to which a support file adheres.

dd.p:
edition date (see Clause 7.31)
name (see Clause 7.50)
version (see Clause 7.92)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: A means or channel of communicating at a distance by electrical or electromagnetic means such as telegraphy, telephony, or broadcasting.

dd.p:
contact instructions (see Clause 7.18)
telecommunication identifier (see Clause 7.83)
telecommunication service (see Clause 7.84)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The temporal interval of the cycle over which data is produced.

dd.p:
type of time interval unit (see Clause 7.88)
value of time (see Clause 7.91)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The temporal interval over which the product is updated or renewed.

dd.p:
expiration date (see Clause 7.33)
issue date (see Clause 7.44)
issuance cycle (see Clause 8.6)

li.p: No remarks.

td.p: IHO Definition: The indication of a specific week within a specific year.

dd.p:
week number (see Clause 7.94)
year number (see Clause 7.95)

li.p: No remarks.
